verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family of sperm-egg fusion proteins, expressed in testis/sperm. Unlike IZUMO1 (single-pass TM), IZUMO4 is reported as a secreted/soluble protein lacking a TM anchor, but it forms a stable complex with other IZUMO family members (IZUMO1) on the sperm surface via disulfide-linked or wash-resistant association. It is displayed on the outer leaflet of sperm during the acrosome reaction in a germline-restricted manner. This is tissue-restricted surface display on a narrow germline lineage, so contextual/tissue_restricted_surface applies. Ruling out other buckets: no cancer/cell-death ecto-pool documented; not lysosomal; no broad somatic PM cycling; complex-partner tethering is the mechanism but the gating signal is germline restriction.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether IZUMO4 stably co-traffics with IZUMO1 on sperm surface or is primarily released as a soluble factor; could alternatively fit stable_complex_partner or secreted_only.
